channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cockroach Janta Party founder alleges website and social media accounts withheld amid legal action in India
item.description: The founder of the online satirical movement Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), Abhijeet Dipke, has claimed that the organisation’s official website and multiple social media accounts have been blocked in I...

item.title: Barangay councilor arrested in Oriental Mindoro over alleged illegal drug involvement
item.description: A village councilor in Oriental Mindoro was arrested in connection with alleged involvement in illegal drugs, according to a report published by ABS-CBN News. The incident highlights ongoing efforts b...

item.title: Nigeria Customs Service Provides Free Healthcare Services to 2,000 Residents in Daura Medical Outreach
item.description: The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) has organised its 7th Retreat Medical Outreach programme in Daura, Katsina State, as part of its community support and social responsibility efforts. The exercise was...

item.title: Prairie Lily riverboat resumes operations in Saskatoon after extended shutdown
item.description: The Prairie Lily riverboat has officially returned to the South Saskatchewan River in Saskatoon after being out of service for nearly 18 months. The popular local attraction resumed operations under n...

item.title: Police respond to reported gunfire near the White House amid heightened security presence
item.description: Law enforcement agencies in the United States are responding to reports of possible gunfire near the White House in Washington, D.C., prompting a significant security response in the area. According t...

item.title: Letters criticise ACT kangaroo cull and a range of public policy concerns
item.description: A series of letters to the editor published in The Canberra Times raises a range of community concerns, led by strong criticism of the ACT’s annual winter kangaroo cull in nature reserves. One corresp...

item.title: Health Department confirms forensic pathology services remain operational during labour dispute
item.description: The National Department of Health has assured the public that forensic pathology services across South Africa are continuing to operate despite sporadic labour-related picketing by some public forensi...

item.title: Hawkstone Farmers' Choir and drone act Celestial reach Britain's Got Talent final after surprise Golden Buzzer twist
item.description: Jeremy Clarkson's Hawkstone Farmers' Choir secured a place in the final of Britain's Got Talent after receiving the highest number of viewer votes during the latest live semi-final. The choir, origina...

item.title: Hull City secure Premier League promotion after McBurnie stoppage-time goal
item.description: Hull City have made a dramatic return to the English Premier League after Oli McBurnie scored a stoppage-time winner in the Championship playoff final against Middlesbrough at Wembley Stadium. The mat...

item.title: Severe rain and wind in southern Ontario force postponement of Bruno Mars Toronto concert
item.description: A major weather system bringing cold, soaking rain and strong winds across southern Ontario has disrupted events in the region, including a scheduled concert by Bruno Mars in Toronto. Organisers annou...

item.title: Jury continues deliberations without verdict in Dean Penney first-degree murder trial
item.description: The jury in the first-degree murder trial of Dean Penney has completed its third full day of deliberations without reaching a verdict. The 12 jurors, sequestered in Corner Brook, Newfoundland and Labr...

item.title: Vintage Fashion Portraits of Model Ludmila Feodoseyevna (“Lud”) in the 1930s and 1940s
item.description: This article presents a curated collection of vintage fashion portraits featuring Ludmila Feodoseyevna, a model known simply as “Lud,” who became prominent in the 1930s and 1940s fashion scene. Born i...

item.title: The 1939 Survival of Boy Scout Donn Fendler on Mount Katahdin
item.description: In July 1939, 12-year-old Donn Fendler from Rye, New York, became the center of a massive search effort after disappearing during a family hike on Mount Katahdin in Maine. While hiking toward the 5,26...

item.title: South Africa selected as Berlinale European Film Market’s 2027 Country in Focus during Cannes Festival
item.description: South Africa has officially been selected as the “Country in Focus” for the 2027 Berlinale European Film Market (EFM), a major international platform linked to the Berlin International Film Festival. ...

item.title: Whole-body vibration training: benefits and scientific evidence on fat loss and muscle strength
item.description: This article examines the growing popularity of whole-body vibration training, a fitness method that involves standing on a vibrating platform for short sessions, typically around 15 minutes several t...

item.title: Ogie Alcasid clarifies Regine Velasquez has no retirement plans yet
item.description: Ogie Alcasid has addressed and clarified recent speculation about the possible retirement of Regine Velasquez, popularly known as Asia’s Songbird and one of the most iconic voices in Original Pilipino...

item.title: Prof. Faith Ken-Aminikpo Delivers Insightful Lecture on Theatre Arts in Education at UNIPORT
item.description: Prof. Faith Ken-Aminikpo, the first female Professor of Theatre Arts in Education from the University of Port Harcourt (UNIPORT) and Rivers State, delivered the institution’s 211th inaugural lecture t...

item.title: Reports of gunfire outside the White House prompt security response
item.description: On Saturday, May 23, 2026, multiple journalists reported hearing what sounded like gunshots outside the White House in Washington, D.C. According to ABC senior White House correspondent Selina Wang, s...

item.title: IAEA Chief Warns UN Security Council of Severe Risks Following Drone Strike Near UAE Nuclear Plant
item.description: International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi addressed the United Nations Security Council following a drone strike incident near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in...

item.title: SolarSquare negotiates $55–60M Series C led by B Capital and Lightspeed amid India rooftop solar growth
item.description: SolarSquare, a Mumbai-based rooftop solar startup founded in 2015, is in advanced discussions to raise a new Series C funding round led by B Capital and Lightspeed Venture Partners. The round is expec...
